title: Ceterach ceterach
intro: Ceterach ceterach, also known as the Alpine Rockcap or Rock Club Moss, is a small, terrestrial fern species that belongs to the family Polypodiaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, promote wound healing, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Ceterach ceterach include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Ceterach ceterach is often used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including skin issues, digestive problems, and respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Ceterach ceterach is classified as a species of fern, specifically within the genus Ceterach. It belongs to the family Polypodiaceae, a group of vascular plants. The genus Ceterach is part of the class Polypodiopsida, which consists of various ferns. Ceterach ceterach is further classified as a flowering plant in the kingdom Plantae, in the domain Eukarya. This classification is based on its morphological and molecular characteristics.
